briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — steady post-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for post-spawn with clear water and stable conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, dock, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Bass realize they can either chase prey and burn energy, or chill out in the shade, burn less energy and eat less. A shaky-head is a good way to pick off bigger fish out of brush piles that
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, dock, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

KVD: 'Bass don't go extremely deep immediately after spawning. They stop at secondary points, road beds, shallow ledges, main lake points in the 8-10ft range. Many anglers pull out deep divers too soon — a medium-diving crankbait in Sexy Shad matc...

Robertson: When following postspawn bass down ditches with a deep diver, start by casting ahead and up on top, then bring the crankbait down the side of the ditch at about a 45-degree angle. The crankbait must hit bottom.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable post-spawn conditions split fish between shallow fry-guarders (2-5ft) and transitioning females (5-15ft). Shad spawn activity on seawalls, riprap, and bluff walls concentrates bigger fish shallow at dawn.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
